proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=97, 93.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +41.4pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8690 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=97, wr=93.7%, edge +41.4pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=97, Wilson lower 0.869, +41.4pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61. Current regime is now Choppy (shifting, day 6) so the Bear-gated cohort is dormant, and portfolio Bear exposure is only 11/77 (14.3%) while the 85.71% concentration trigger is on Choppy — promotion does not compound the flagged concentration. Consistent with continuous prior approvals (2026-04-29 through 2026-06-20) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: BULL_PROXY × Bear: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=BULL_PROXY, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +28.2pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.98 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
Regime has shifted back to Choppy so cohort is currently inactive; activation deferred until Bear returns
BULL_PROXY open exposure is only 3/77 — small base for further BULL_PROXY skew, but TAKE_SMALL sizing limits risk
approval would propagate redundant to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +28.2pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +28.2pp edge over BULL_PROXY baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.98 (<1.0). Cohort is regime-gated to Bear but current regime is now Choppy (shifting, day 6); portfolio Bear exposure is 11/77 (14.3%) while the 85.71% concentration trigger is on Choppy, so a Bear-gated BULL_PROXY promotion does not compound flagged concentration and may diversify when Bear re-emerges. Consistent with prior approvals of this exact cohort on 2026-06-19 and 2026-06-20 and adjacent UP_TRI×Bear approvals; no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_regime + by_signal_type = Choppy regime (85.71%) + UP_TRI signal (92.2%) (85.7%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_regime + by_signal_type = Choppy regime (85.71%) + UP_TRI signal (92.2%); recommended action: Pause new UP_TRI entries in Choppy regime until either (a) cohort_health Choppy×UP_TRI promotes to Tier M, or (b) regime share drops below 0.80 via natural Day-6 exits. Prioritize letting Bank, FMCG, and Pharma positions roll off before adding any new long exposure in those sectors.
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: A Choppy→Bear regime flip unwinds the 96.1% long-directional book simultaneously with no statistical edge backing the dominant Choppy×UP_TRI cohort; sector clusters in Bank (12), FMCG (10), and Pharma (10) compound the drawdown as cross-sector correlation spikes in a risk-off shift.

evidence: Matched cohort Choppy×UP_TRI at Candidate tier (n=234) — unvalidated edge
Top cells UP_TRI×Choppy×Bank 15.58%, FMCG 11.69%, Pharma 11.69% — all under 0.20 cell threshold but compounding regime/signal concentration
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new UP_TRI entries in Choppy regime until either (a) cohort_health Choppy×UP_TRI promotes to Tier M, or (b) regime share drops below 0.80 via natural Day-6 exits. Prioritize letting Bank, FMCG, and Pharma positions roll off before adding any new long exposure in those sectors.
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: A Choppy→Bear regime flip unwinds the 96.1% long-directional book simultaneously with no statistical edge backing the dominant Choppy×UP_TRI cohort; sector clusters in Bank (12), FMCG (10), and Pharma (10) compound the drawdown as cross-sector correlation spikes in a risk-off shift.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: Regime concentration triggered at 0.8571 (Choppy, above 0.80 threshold) with matched Choppy×UP_TRI cohort only at Candidate tier (n=234) — unvalidated edge. UP_TRI also dominates signal mix at 71/77 = 92.2% (above 0.85 threshold) with the book overwhelmingly directional long (UP_TRI + BULL_PROXY = 74/77 = 96.1%). No single cell breaches the 0.20 threshold (top cell Bank at 15.58%), but the regime + signal-type concentration on unvalidated cohort is the actionable risk.
